nodes.text: [stage:frasier stool][stage:john counter_post][shot:insert phone]Frasier turned the phone screen toward him. On the screen, a sequence of geometric shapes pulsed in slow rotation, blue and green and a color John had never seen on a screen before, a kind of luminous bruise that did not seem to belong to the visible spectrum. The shapes were, perhaps, a city seen from above. The shapes were, perhaps, a circuit. The shapes were, perhaps, both.
"Working on a new model city myself. More — resilient. Thought you should see."
"Because someone has to be the witness, Johnny. That's your job. I appreciate that you take it seriously."
"I appreciate that you take it more seriously than you should."

[shot:insert notebook]John looked at his own notebook, open beside the coffee mug. Filled with cryptic observations, overheard snippets, hand-drawn diagrams of Graustark's warped ley lines. He was trying to document the decay. Frasier was, evidently, building new decays from scratch.
Architect of Decay, John had scribbled once, in the margin, next to Frasier's name. He had circled it. He still wasn't sure if it was a job title or a warning label.
"Maybe the story isn't about understanding it. Maybe it's just about — surviving it."
Frasier clapped him on the shoulder. The contact was a jolt that vibrated through John's weary bones, a jolt that felt suspiciously like a small, deliberate transmission.
Hello, the jolt seemed to say, on a frequency below speech. Hello. Stay alert. Sorry about what's coming.
"Surviving? Where's the fun in that? The trick isn't to survive the fall, Johnny. It's to learn how to fly on the way down."
[stage:frasier kitchen_door][shot:closeup frasier_door]He paused at the door. Hand on the push bar. A final pronouncement waiting to be delivered.
"Or," Frasier said, "at the very least — learn how to write about the landing."
[stage:frasier off][shot:establish]He was gone. The kitchen door swung once, twice, and stilled. The smell of burnt sugar lingered for a long time, and then began, slowly, to leave.
